children.text: the Government of the Russian Federation bears responsibility for the continuing violence in Eastern Ukraine, including the death on , of Joseph Stone, a citizen of the United States working as a monitor for the Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe;April 24, 2017

children.label: A
children.text: to withdraw all of its forces from the territories of Georgia, Ukraine, and Moldova;

children.label: B
children.text: to return control of the borders of those territories to their respective governments; and

children.label: C
children.text: to cease all efforts to undermine the popularly elected governments of those countries;

the Government of the Russian Federation has applied, and continues to apply, to the countries and peoples of Georgia and Ukraine, traditional uses of force, intelligence operations, and influence campaigns, which represent clear and present threats to the countries of Europe and Eurasia;
in response, the countries of Europe and Eurasia should redouble efforts to build resilience within their institutions, political systems, and civil societies;
the United States supports the institutions that the Government of the Russian Federation seeks to undermine, including the North Atlantic Treaty Organization and the European Union;
a strong North Atlantic Treaty Organization is critical to maintaining peace and security in Europe and Eurasia;

the United States should continue to work with the European Union as a partner against aggression by the Government of the Russian Federation, coordinating aid programs, development assistance, and other counter-Russian efforts;

the United States should encourage the establishment of a commission for media freedom within the Council of Europe, modeled on the Venice Commission regarding rule of law issues, that would be chartered to provide governments with expert recommendations on maintaining legal and regulatory regimes supportive of free and independent media and an informed citizenry able to distinguish between fact-based reporting, opinion, and disinformation;

children.label: A
children.text: to identify vulnerabilities to aggression, disinformation, corruption, and so-called hybrid warfare by the Government of the Russian Federation;

children.label: B
children.text: to establish strategic and technical plans for addressing those vulnerabilities;

children.label: C
children.text: to ensure that the financial systems of those countries are not being used to shield illicit financial activity by officials of the Government of the Russian Federation or individuals in President Vladimir Putin’s inner circle who have been enriched through corruption;

children.label: D
children.text: to investigate and prosecute cases of corruption by Russian actors; and

children.label: E
children.text: to work toward full compliance with the Convention on Combating Bribery of Foreign Public Officials in International Business Transactions (commonly referred to as the “Anti-Bribery Convention”) of the Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development; and

children.label: A
children.text: the Sergei Magnitsky Rule of Law Accountability Act of 2012 (title IV of ; note); andPublic Law 112–20822 U.S.C. 5811

children.label: B
children.text: the Global Magnitsky Human Rights Accountability Act (subtitle F of title XII of ; note).Public Law 114–32822 U.S.C. 26561See References in Text note below.1
